perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is understood to be proprietary in nature.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential' to 'is understood to be proprietary in nature' significantly alters the scope of what constitutes 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction because the original definition includes an objective standard (what the Recipient *should* reasonably know), while the modified version implies a subjective standard (what is 'understood'). The Contradiction takes place in section 4, since it uses the concept of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' in this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and not for any third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose. Each party agrees that it will not disclose any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the express written consent of Owner. Neither party shall reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any of the Confidential Information of the other party or any of the prototypes, software or other tangible objects which embody the Confidential Information of the other party and which are provided to the party hereunder. Certain Confidential Information may be considered material non-public information under Regulation FD promulgated by the Securities and Exchange Commission. Recipient acknowledges that the United States securities laws prohibit it or any person who has received material nonpublic information about the Owner from purchasing or selling securities of the Owner or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance on such information, and agrees not to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information until such time as the information becomes disseminated to the public either through a press release issued by Owner or through a filing made by Owner pursuant to the Securities CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 1 of 3 Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; provided, however, that Recipient may make any disclosure or use of such information to which Owner gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' using an objective standard ('should reasonably be known'), while the changed text introduces a subjective standard ('is understood'). This creates a contradiction because Section 4 relies on a clear definition of 'Confidential Information' to establish obligations regarding its use and disclosure. The change from an objective to a subjective standard significantly alters the scope of what is considered 'Confidential Information,' creating uncertainty and potential disputes about whether certain information falls under the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 4. Therefore, the altered definition directly contradicts the enforceability and understanding of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: If Recipient is required to disclose Owner’s Confidential Information pursuant to a valid order by a court or other governmental body or as otherwise required by law, prior to any such compelled disclosure, Recipient will (i) notify Owner of the legal process, and allow Owner to assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Confidential Information against the third party seeking disclosure and (ii) reasonably cooperate with Owner in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure and/or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If Recipient is mandated to disclose Owner’s Confidential Information pursuant to a valid order by a court or other governmental body or as otherwise dictated by law, prior to any such compelled disclosure, Recipient will (i) notify Owner of the legal process, and Owner may elect to assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Confidential Information against the third party seeking disclosure and (ii) shall not be required to reasonably cooperate with Owner in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure and/or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Recipient will reasonably cooperate with Owner' to 'shall not be required to reasonably cooperate with Owner' creates a direct contradiction in Recipient's obligations in case of legally compelled disclosure. This introduces uncertainty on what happens if there is a legal action against the recipient that requires disclosure. The contradiction takes place in section 11, since if a breach of agreement takes place on section 3, the section 11 specifies the actions to take place, but now it will have a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the other party for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, the harmed party may be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that if Owner's Confidential Information needs to be disclosed by Recipient, then Recipient 'will reasonably cooperate with Owner'. The changed text modifies this obligation such that Recipient 'shall not be required to reasonably cooperate with Owner'. This creates a direct conflict with the remedies specified in the contradicted text. If the recipient does not need to cooperate then there is no breach of agreement, but if there is no breach of agreement, section 11 is unnecessary and creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may optionally limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to limit' to 'may optionally limit' weakens the obligation to limit disclosure. This creates a contradiction. The original text makes limiting disclosures a mandatory action, the modification now makes it optional. The contradictory effect is on section 5, since the obligation of the recipient is being compromised due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality. Each party agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information and shall ensure that its employees and agents who have access to Confidential Information of the other party are subject to confidentiality obligations similar in scope and nature to the provisions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees or agents. Each party shall reproduce the other party’s proprietary rights notices on any such approved copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires each party to 'limit disclosures' while the changed text makes it optional ('may optionally limit disclosures'). This directly contradicts the 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' section, which states 'Each party agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure'. Making the limitation optional undermines the agreement to take reasonable measures to avoid disclosure, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This follows the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, as the obligation of the recipient is being compromised due to this change.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it shall endeavor to take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'endeavor' weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. While seemingly minor, 'endeavor' implies an effort but not a guarantee, contrasting with the original's stronger commitment. The contradiction can be seen in the remedies section. If the recipient fails to protect and only endeavors to protect there is a contradictoion between the failure and the responsability for the damage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the other party for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, the harmed party may be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party 'shall take reasonable measures', indicating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation by adding 'endeavor to', implying an effort but not a guarantee. This directly contradicts the 'Remedies' section, which assumes that a breach of the agreement will cause irreparable harm and seeks to remedy it with equitable relief. If a party only 'endeavors' to protect confidential information and fails, the remedies section is undermined because it's unclear if the failure constitutes a breach justifying the remedies. The added language therefore creates a strong contradiction by weakening the protective obligation, making enforcement and the application of remedies ambiguous. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner and shall be promptly returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request or upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, are not required to be promptly returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request or upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be promptly returned' to 'are not required to be promptly returned' creates a direct contradiction regarding the return of materials. This introduces uncertainty and makes the obligation unenforceable. This change contradicts the confidentiality obligations outlined in section 5. The original wording implies immediate action to preserve confidentiality by returning materials, while the modification suggests otherwise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality. Each party agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information and shall ensure that its employees and agents who have access to Confidential Information of the other party are subject to confidentiality obligations similar in scope and nature to the provisions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees or agents. Each party shall reproduce the other party’s proprietary rights notices on any such approved copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that documents containing Confidential Information are 'not required to be promptly returned', directly contradicting the 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' clause, which mandates 'reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information'. By removing the requirement to return confidential information, the changed text undermines the obligation to maintain confidentiality, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
